mission: Learn transferable production-agent engineering by reproducing failures, naming contracts, choosing deliberately, and proving repairs.

phases.name: Measure
phases.promise: Trust the instrument and define the service promise.

phases.name: Isolate
phases.promise: Make sessions concurrent without crossing tenant boundaries.

phases.name: Operate under pressure
phases.promise: Control state, demand, provider failure, and spend.

phases.name: Run as a service
phases.promise: Observe, deploy, and roll out the agent safely.

phases.name: Define agent behavior
phases.promise: Make contracts, authority, and coordination explicit.

phases.name: Survive production reality
phases.promise: Govern context, hosting, and durable execution.

knowledge_checks: Which measurement proves a full conversation traversed the real public API?
What false confidence would a mocked inner loop create?
Which product promise does each SLO protect, and for what workload boundary?
What evidence distinguishes queueing collapse from a merely slow model?
What must remain ordered inside one session while other sessions progress?
Where can concurrency be introduced without duplicating effects?
Where is tenant identity constructed, and why can the model not choose it?
Which stores, tools, traces, and meters must a leak test cover?
Which state must survive process loss, and which state may remain ephemeral?
What query or migration fails first at the next order of magnitude?
Where should overload be rejected so work does not enter the system dishonestly?
How do deadlines and retries interact when the queue is already saturated?
Which failures are safe to retry, and what budget stops amplification?
What evidence tells a real recovery from a fallback that violates the contract?
Where must a shared budget be enforced to cover model and tool work together?
What happens to an in-flight run when its tenant budget is exhausted?
Which correlation identifiers let you follow one accepted request across boundaries?
How can telemetry localize failure without leaking prompt or tenant content?
Which accepted work becomes unsafe if a worker disappears mid-request?
What hidden affinity would make a rolling restart lose correctness?
Which offline and live signals should automatically block promotion?
How do shadow and canary traffic answer different rollout questions?
At which boundaries must invalid agent data fail explicitly?
What compatibility promise does a contract version make to producers and consumers?
Which values may inform a decision but must never grant capability?
Where must approval be verified so prompt injection cannot forge it?
Which handoff fields make specialist work inspectable and resumable?
Where is the terminal state decided when specialists disagree or exhaust budget?
What provenance, freshness, and scope must accompany retrieved context?
Which write path could let generated text poison future decisions?
Which startup and shutdown behaviors must be proved inside the immutable image?
What local dependency would fail under non-root and read-only production constraints?
After which side effects can a worker die, and what guarantee holds at each point?
What idempotency key and durable state allow duplicate delivery to resume safely?
